title: [PLACEHOLDER] AI-Native Consulting Delivery: Rebuilding the Engagement Model Around Agent Fleets
abstract: A framework for restructuring consulting delivery around parallel AI agent fleets rather than linear staffing pyramids. Covers ticket decomposition, review-gate discipline, and where human judgment remains the bottleneck by design.

title: [PLACEHOLDER] Mapping the Vegas Tech Corridor: Capital, Talent, and Gaps
abstract: A survey of Las Vegas's emerging technology sector — funding flows, talent migration patterns, and the specific gaps a locally rooted consulting practice is positioned to fill. Includes a comparison against Austin and Phoenix at equivalent growth stages.

title: [PLACEHOLDER] Ticket-Driven Development as a Governance Layer for Autonomous Agents
abstract: How pairing GitHub Issues, a dependency waterfall, and a review-gate board turns loosely supervised AI agents into an auditable production pipeline. Written as a running case study of VCP's own build process.

title: [PLACEHOLDER] Financial Modeling for Single-Practitioner and Small-Team Consulting Firms
abstract: A practical modeling approach for firms too small for enterprise FP&A tooling but too complex for spreadsheet guesswork — cash runway, utilization rate, and pipeline-weighted revenue forecasting in one reusable template.

title: [PLACEHOLDER] The Open-in-Public Transparency Model: Publishing Work as the Record
abstract: Why VCP publishes its projects, research, and papers as they are produced rather than after the fact, and what that discipline costs and returns — trust velocity, recruiting signal, and the operational overhead of always being publish-ready.
